content:
type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Add optional keyword-only parameter 'transform_callback' to exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, transform_callback=None). Signature: transform_callback: Callable[[int], Transpose | Sequence[Transpose] | None] | None = None. Behavior: after reading 'orientation = image_exif.get(ExifTags.Base.Orientation, 1)', if transform_callback is not None, call 'method = transform_callback(orientation)' instead of the default dict lookup. The callback receives the raw integer orientation value (1-8) and returns either: (a) a single Image.Transpose enum value (same as the default dict values: FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT, ROTATE_180, FLIP_TOP_BOTTOM, TRANSPOSE, ROTATE_270, TRANSVERSE, ROTATE_90), (b) a sequence/list of such Transpose values to be applied in order (each applied via successive image.transpose(m) / image.im.transpose(m) calls, chaining the result), or (c) None, meaning no transform should be applied (equivalent to orientation 1 in the default mapping). If transform_callback is None (the default), fall back exactly to the existing behavior (the fixed orientation->method dict). This must work correctly with the existing in_place True/False branches, and the existing EXIF-orientation-tag-removal logic below must still run whenever a transform was actually applied (method is not None, or a non-empty sequence was returned). Add type import Callable and Sequence from collections.abc / typing as needed. Please add tests to Tests/test_imageops.py exercising: (1) transform_callback returning a single Transpose value matches default mapping result for a given orientation, (2) transform_callback returning a sequence of Transpose values applies them in order, (3) transform_callback returning None results in no transposition (image unchanged, orientation tag still removed), (4) omitting transform_callback preserves existing default behavior (regression), (5) transform_callback takes precedence over the default mapping even for orientations 2-8. I will not edit ImageOps.py or test_imageops.py myself - you own both files entirely.
note: transform_callback overrides default orientation->method mapping; supports single Transpose, list of Transpose, or None
